pages.text: good evening west orange and welcome to our final council meeting of 2019. i hope you're all keeping warm and roasty toasty and madam clerk let's open a meeting this is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with section 5 of the open public meetings act chapter 231 public law 1975. the annual notice was emailed to the star ledger and filed in the township clerk's office on september 26 2018 and published in the west orange chronicle on october 4th 2018. councilwoman casolino president councilman krokoviak president councilwoman tooke brown president councilwoman mccartney council president guerino president thank you thank you madam clerk okay todd cohen couldn't be here tonight but he was supposed to be here last meeting but he represents and he's very active in the research and the fight for pancreatic cancer so i'm not going to get up at the podium and and read the mayor's proclamation but if you allow me i'll just

take a few minutes to read it to you something is very important something it's another issue of health and what we need to do to try to fight this dreaded disease that claims so many people so quickly and so fast whereas in 2019 an estimated 55 400 people will be diagnosed with pancreatic cancer in the united states and 44 330 will die from this disease whereas pancreatic cancer is one of the deadliest cancers is currently the third leading cause of cancer deaths in the united states and is projected to become the second leading cause around twenty twenty and whereas pancreatic cancer is the only major cancer with a five-year relative survival rate in the single digits at just nine percent whereas when symptoms of pancreatic cancer present themselves it is generally in the later stages and only 10 percent of pancreatic cancer patients are diagnosed when the disease is confirmed to their pancreas and 91 percent of pancreatic cancer patients die within the first five years whereas approximately 1

290 deaths will occur in new jersey in 2019 and whereas pancreatic cancer is the seventh most common cause of cancer-related deaths across the world and whereas there will be an estimated 480 480 000 new pancreatic cancer cases diagnosed worldwide in 2020 whereas the good health and well-being of the residents of west orange are enhanced as a direct result of increased awareness about the symptoms and risk of pancreatic cancer and research into early detection causes and effective treatments and therefore be resolved that the township designates november 21st 2019 as world pancreatic cancer during the cat throughout the township west orange respectively submitted on behalf of mayor robert d parisi mayor of the township west orange thank you and i'm sure we'll get this to todd and he does a fantastic job and his group and of getting the awareness out and research and raising funds for this really dreaded cancer and a lot of us here in the council know personally people who are affected by and who were affected by it 91 00:05:01,
